retrieved_docs.title: How To Read A Scientific Manuscript -- Clinical Significance -- Literature Search
retrieved_docs.content: The next step is to search for existing literature based on your research objective. Specific tactics for conducting a thorough literature search are not covered in this topic, but using multiple databases—such as OVID, PubMed, and Google Scholar—is essential for an effective literature search. [1] [2] When searching scientific databases, you can filter studies by factors such as publication year, subject type (human or animal), and article type (such as trials or reviews), which helps narrow and target your search.

retrieved_docs.title: Use and Application of MAUDE In Patient Safety -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: After filling out the relevant information, anywhere from 5 to 100 records can be displayed per page. The data for the desired dates can be downloaded in the Microsoft Excel format, which allows users to access the data offline as well. The search allows a maximum of only 500 records to be returned from a single search entry. If more results are desired, 2 separate searches by putting 2 different dates will be required. In the online MAUDE database query form, several different keyword combinations may need to be entered to capture the maximum number of results available. Furthermore, the device field entry may be combined with the manufacturer field entry to capture accurate data.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 2230
retrieved_docs.content: Medical databases are clinically associated with a variety of obstetric and perinatal conditions. They contain information on الْتِهابُ المَشيماءِ والسَّلَى, تقلص الحوض, تقلص مخرج الحوض, تقلص مدخل الحوض, قلة السائل الأمنيوسي, نواتج متبقية من الحمل, and gestation twin. By linking these entities, databases support the identification, monitoring, and management of these complications, facilitating evidence‑based decision‑making in maternal‑fetal medicine.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 5 -- Community 2924
retrieved_docs.content: Databases are clinically associated with gestation twin, indicating that information systems and data repositories are linked to the clinical aspects and management of twin pregnancies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 202
retrieved_docs.content: care. Collectively, these entities form an integrated network of obstetric complications, procedural interventions, positional techniques, multiple‑gestation classifications, neonatal screening considerations, and database linkages that support comprehensive maternal‑fetal risk assessment, research, and optimal patient‑care strategies in obstetrics.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 4 -- Community 2948
retrieved_docs.content: الزَرَق (SMQ) is a member of بضع الزاوية, indicating that the standardized medical query الزَرَق is classified within the بضع الزاوية grouping.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 4 -- Community 2858
retrieved_docs.content: Databases are clinically associated with نواتج متبقية من الحمل and with gestation twin, indicating that medical information systems are linked to the monitoring and analysis of residual pregnancy outcomes as well as twin gestations, thereby supporting clinical decision‑making and research in obstetrics.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 4 -- Community 2910
retrieved_docs.content: CPTAC Terminology, a specialized biomedical vocabulary, includes the concept of غسيل الكلى غير محدد (unspecified dialysis), indicating that within this terminology set there is a designated term for dialysis procedures that are not otherwise specified. This inclusion supports standardized annotation and reporting of dialysis events when detailed information is unavailable, thereby enhancing consistency in data capture and analysis across proteomic and clinical research contexts.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 1949
retrieved_docs.content: Databases function as clinical resources that are associated with الْتِهابُ المَشيماءِ والسَّلَى, with نواتج متبقية من الحمل, and with gestation twin. This indicates that the databases contain relevant information on the condition الْتِهابُ المَشيماءِ والسَّلَى (chorioamnionitis and sepsis), on the residual outcomes that may follow a pregnancy (نواتج متبقية من الحمل), and on twin gestations (gestation twin). By linking these entities, the databases support medical understanding and patient care related to infectious complications of pregnancy, post‑pregnancy sequelae, and the management of twin pregnancies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 1 -- Community 161
retrieved_docs.content: scarring into a unified classification scheme that supports recognition, documentation, and management of pregnancy‑related complications across multiple languages and clinical decision‑making pathways.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 1 -- Community 133
retrieved_docs.content: form a structured taxonomy that aids clinicians in categorizing vascular interventions, selecting appropriate devices, aligning procedural documentation with standardized ontologies, and managing associated monitoring and thrombotic complications in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 3 -- Community 1910
retrieved_docs.content: Databases serve as important clinical resources that are associated with a range of obstetric conditions. They contain relevant information on الْتِهابُ المَشيماءِ والسَّلَى (chorioamnionitis), a serious infection of the fetal membranes that can affect maternal and fetal outcomes. They also provide data on تسمم الحمل (preeclampsia), a hypertensive disorder of pregnancy that poses risks of organ damage and requires careful monitoring. In addition, databases include information on حَمْلٌ بُوقِيّ (bicornuate pregnancy, a uterine anomaly that can influence fetal development and delivery planning). Finally, they cover قَيْء الحمل خفيف الوطأة (mild hyperemesis gravidarum), a condition characterized by moderate nausea and vomiting during pregnancy that may require supportive care. By integrating knowledge on these conditions, databases support clinicians in diagnosis, management, and research aimed at improving maternal‑fetal health.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 2 -- Community 531
retrieved_docs.content: Databases are clinically associated with مقدمات تسمم الحمل (the introductory material on preeclampsia), الْتِهابُ المَشيماءِ والسَّلَى (inflammation of the placenta and membranes, or chorioamnionitis), and تسمم الحمل (preeclampsia). Newborn Screening Terminology includes concepts that are subsets of مقدمات تسمم الحمل and تسمم الحمل, indicating that the terminology used in newborn screening encompasses the introductory aspects of preeclampsia as well as the condition itself. This interconnection underscores the relevance of both databases and newborn screening terminology to the understanding, identification, and clinical management of these obstetric disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 1 -- Community 236
retrieved_docs.content: السَّلَوِيّ). Together these entities and hierarchical relationships provide a unified ontology for risk assessment, diagnosis, and intervention throughout pregnancy, labor, and the postpartum period.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 1 -- Community 93
retrieved_docs.content: abnormalities, treatment‑related complications, and a wide array of multilingual disease entities into a comprehensive classification system that supports accurate diagnosis, coding, reporting, and patient management across diverse medical domains.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 1756
retrieved_docs.content: The term “اضطراب جنيني” (fetal disorder) is mapped from “متلازمة الضائقة الجنينية” (fetal distress syndrome), indicating that the clinical entity described as fetal distress syndrome corresponds to or is represented by the label fetal disorder in certain contexts. This mapping clarifies terminology, ensuring consistent reference to the same condition across medical documentation and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 1491
retrieved_docs.content: Medical databases serve as clinically relevant resources for a range of obstetric conditions. They are linked to the study and management of الْتِهابُ المَشيماءِ والسَّلَى (inflammation of the placenta and membranes), حَمْلٌ بُوقِيّ (a specific type of abnormal pregnancy), قلة السائل الأمنيوسي (oligohydramnios), نواتج متبقية من الحمل (retained products of conception), and gestation twin (twin pregnancy). By aggregating data on these entities, databases support diagnosis, risk assessment, and therapeutic decision‑making in maternal‑fetal medicine.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 2341
retrieved_docs.content: The medical grouping “الكَلَف” includes as one of its members the SMQ titled “حالات الحمل الطبيعي ونتائجه (SMQ)”, which covers natural pregnancy cases and their outcomes. This relationship indicates that the SMQ for natural pregnancy outcomes is classified within the broader “الكَلَف” category, underscoring its relevance for organizing and interpreting pregnancy‑related clinical information.
